name: Layers
summary: Note: As of SketchUp 2020 “Layers” were renamed to “Tags” in the UI. The API retains the use of “Layer” for compatibility and is synonymous with “Tag”. The Layers collection allows you to see and manage all of the layers in a model. You get a pointer to the Layers object from within the Model.

methods.summary: The #[] method is used to retrieve a layer by index or name.
methods.remarks: The #[] method is used to retrieve a layer by index or name.

params.name: index_or_name
params.doc: A number representing the layer's index in an array of Layer objects, or the name of the layer.

methods.name: add
methods.signature: add(layer_name) => Sketchup::Layer Also known as: add_layer
methods.summary: The #add method is used to add a new layer.
methods.remarks: The #add method is used to add a new layer. If you give the name of a Layer that is already defined, it will return the existing Layer rather than adding a new one.

params.name: layer_name
params.doc: The name of the added layer.

methods.name: add_folder
methods.summary: The #add_folder method adds or moves a layer folder.
methods.remarks: The #add_folder method adds or moves a layer folder.

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The #add_observer method is used to add an observer to the layers collection.
methods.remarks: The #add_observer method is used to add an observer to the layers collection.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: at
methods.summary: The #at method is an alias for #[].
methods.remarks: The #at method is an alias for #[].

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length. Returns integer - the number of layers in the collection.

returns.doc: integer - the number of layers in the collection

methods.name: count_folders
methods.summary: The #count_folders method counts the number of folders which are direct children of the layer manager.
methods.remarks: The #count_folders method counts the number of folders which are direct children of the layer manager.

methods.name: count_layers
methods.summary: The #count_layers method retrieves the number of layers not in a folder.
methods.remarks: The #count_layers method retrieves the number of layers not in a folder.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each.
methods.remarks: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each. This would change the size of the collection and cause elements to be skipped as the indices change. Instead copy the current collection to an array using to_a and then use each on the array, when removing content. The #each method is used to iterate through all of the layers in the model. This include layers that are nested inside folders.

methods.name: each_folder
methods.summary: The #each_folder method is used to iterate through the folders that are direct children to the layer manager.
methods.remarks: The #each_folder method is used to iterate through the folders that are direct children to the layer manager.

methods.name: each_layer
methods.summary: The #each_layer method is used to iterate through the layers that are not inside a layer folder.
methods.remarks: The #each_layer method is used to iterate through the layers that are not inside a layer folder.

methods.name: folders
methods.summary: Note: This does not return all the folders in the model, only those that are direct children of the layer manager.
methods.remarks: Note: This does not return all the folders in the model, only those that are direct children of the layer manager. The #folders method returns the folders of the layer manager.

methods.name: layers
methods.summary: The #layers method retrieves the layers not in a folder.
methods.remarks: The #layers method retrieves the layers not in a folder.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method retrieves the number of layers.
methods.remarks: The #length method retrieves the number of layers.

methods.name: purge_unused
methods.signature: purge_unused => Integer Also known as: purge_unused_layers
methods.summary: The #purge_unused method is used to remove unused layers.
methods.remarks: The #purge_unused method is used to remove unused layers.

methods.name: purge_unused_folders
methods.summary: The #purge_unused_folders method is used to remove all layer folder with no children.
methods.remarks: The #purge_unused_folders method is used to remove all layer folder with no children.

methods.name: remove
methods.signature: remove(layer, remove_geometry = false) => Boolean Also known as: remove_layer
methods.summary: Remove the given layer from the model, optionally removing the geometry.
methods.remarks: Remove the given layer from the model, optionally removing the geometry.

params.name: remove_geometry
params.doc: If true, geometry in the removed layer will be removed as well. If false (which is the default), this geometry will be placed on Layer 0.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: remove_folder
methods.summary: The #remove_folder method removes the folder from the model.
methods.remarks: The #remove_folder method removes the folder from the model. All children are preserved, but moved up one level.

throws.when: if the folder is not a direct child of the receiver.

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The #remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The #remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is an alias of #length.
methods.remarks: The #size method is an alias of #length.

methods.name: unique_name
methods.summary: The #unique_name method can be used to get a string that will be a unique layer name inside this collection.
methods.remarks: The #unique_name method can be used to get a string that will be a unique layer name inside this collection.

params.name: base_name
params.doc: The base name to build the unique name from.

returns.doc: Will default to using “Layer” (SketchUp2019 and older) or “Tag” as basename for a unique name.
